content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

July 29, 1981

Mr. Robert S. McCarter
6336 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, California 90048

Dear Mr. McCarter:

Thank you very much for your letter dated July 20, 1981.
This will verify my advice to you during our telephone
conversation of July 28, 1981 regarding the reporting of a
check received from the California Mortgage Bankers
Association which you wrote of in your letter.

The facts as I understand them to be are as follows:

1. The California Mortgage Bankers Association issued
a check in the amount of $10,250 to CAMPAC, the political
action committee of the California Mortgage Banking Industry.

2. The check represents contributions of $25 to $50
from individuals who attended a "Las Vegas Night" held during
a recent convention of the Association, and,

3. No single contributor contributed a hundred dollars
or more. The individual contribution slips and pertinent
documentation are at the Association's office.

The question you asked was, how do you report the $10,250
check from the Association?

I advised that you had the option of reporting the check in
either of two methods.

1. Either report the $10,250 as a total lump sum of
contribution received less than $100 for the period covered,
or,

2. Itemize the check as coming from the Association by name
and address with an additional description or explanation that
this check represents the proceeds from the "Las Vegas Night"
event held on whatever date it was held, and, further, that
no single contributor contributed $100 or more.

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

Mr. Robert S. McCarter
Page Two
July 29, 1981

I also advised that the individual contributor slips are to
be made part of the Treasurer's records in order to substantiate
the fact that all contributions received were less than $100.

I hope this information has been helpful to you. Please
do not hesitate to contact our office again if we can be of
any further assistance.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Arriola
Political Reform
Consultant

HPA:jg

sections.question: How do you report the $10,250 check from the Association?
sections.conclusion: I advised that you had the option of reporting the check in either of two methods. 1. Either report the $10,250 as a total lump sum of contribution received less than $100 for the period covered, or, 2. Itemize the check as coming from the Association by name and address with an additional description or explanation that this check represents the proceeds from the 'Las Vegas Night' event held on whatever date it was held, and, further, that no single contributor contributed $100 or more. I also advised that the individual contributor slips are to be made part of the Treasurer's records in order to substantiate the fact that all contributions received were less than $100.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked how to properly report a $10,250 check received by CAMPAC (a political action committee) from the California Mortgage Bankers Association, where the check represents pooled contributions of $25-$50 from individuals at a 'Las Vegas Night' event, with no single contributor giving $100 or more.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advised that the $10,250 check could be reported either as a lump sum of contributions under $100 or itemized by the Association with an explanation that proceeds came from the Las Vegas Night event where no individual contributor gave $100 or more. Individual contributor slips must be retained as Treasurer's records to substantiate that all contributions were below the $100 threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers present, but question and conclusion text are clearly demarcated in the letter body. Document is a responsive advice letter with legible content.

QUESTION: How do you report the $10,250 check from the Association?

CONCLUSION: I advised that you had the option of reporting the check in either of two methods. 1. Either report the $10,250 as a total lump sum of contribution received less than $100 for the period covered, or, 2. Itemize the check as coming from the Association by name and address with an additional description or explanation that this check represents the proceeds from the 'Las Vegas Night' event held on whatever date it was held, and, further, that no single contributor contributed $100 or more. I also advised that the individual contributor slips are to be made part of the Treasurer's records in order to substantiate the fact that all contributions received were less than $100.

embedding.summary: FPPC response letter from July 1981 advising Robert S. McCarter on how to properly report a $10,250 pooled contribution check received by CAMPAC from the California Mortgage Bankers Association, providing two acceptable reporting methods.
